reasoning: Evidence suggests Climate.gov is more likely than not to remain online through July 1, 2025. Key factors: (1) An immediate shutdown threat on April 4, 2025 received a 'reprieve with contract extension' the same day (search_3_summary); (2) NOAA has continued to actively publish new content on the site as recently as May 2, 2025 (search_1_summary); (3) The site was confirmed UP on May 7, 2025 (background); (4) NOAA added statutory authority language citing Congressional mandates in March (search_1_summary), potentially providing legal protection. While content censorship has occurred (Climate Literacy Guide removed in February - search_1_summary), the website infrastructure appears to have survived the most acute threat period. The 40-day window from knowledge cutoff (May 21) to resolution (July 1) is relatively short, and the site has demonstrated operational continuity through mid-May. The main risk is renewed contract/funding issues, but no specific new threats are evident.
